title: [CI:BUILD] rpm: hard dependency on gvisor-tap-vsock-gvforwarder
body:
With https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/pull/268, gvforwarder is now provided via the subpackage
`gvisor-tap-vsock-gvforwarder`. FCOS needs this gvforwarder, hence the hard dependency.

gvisor-tap-vsock is a separate package for f38, so the subpackage conditional has been adjusted to fit that.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @martinpitt revdeps failed. seeing this elsewhere?

whoops ignore me. Looks like it's because of the change I made.
The revdep tests should pass once the gvisor-tap-vsock change is merged.

@lsm5: Yes, "nothing provides gvisor-tap-vsock-gvforwarder". Nevertheless, big thanks for the notification! Wrt. that, automatic notifications have a high chance of landing next Tuesday :tada: https://github.com/packit/packit-service/pull/2182
We don't need to make the change for f37. So, I'll revise this PR a bit.
the revdep failure should go away once https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/pull/271 is merged. I didn't see any conflict with the rpms built on that PR and the one in fedora.
the gvisor PR has been merged. I'll wait a while and rerun the revdep tests here to ensure we're green.
really weird why the rpm conflict still occurs on f39 but not on rawhide and f38. Looking..

@lsm5 : For the [F38 run](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/e3b3a2cd-e327-40db-896a-ea6775ef2296/guest-setup-2cb10df1-c2d2-4882-ba39-d983a16adaa5/artifact-installation-2cb10df1-c2d2-4882-ba39-d983a16adaa5/4-Install-packages.txt) , vsock-gvforwarder was taken from your COPR:
```
 gvisor-tap-vsock-gvforwarder   x86_64  103:0.7.0-1.20230914182756041449.main.39.g9ebd937                copr:copr.fedorainfracloud.org:rhcontainerbot:podman-next  1.8 M
```

but for the [F39 run](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/8ad9974d-687d-4646-a49a-d3b5437dd566/guest-setup-a2baee97-21e7-475a-90f0-7c920b59c8cf/artifact-installation-a2baee97-21e7-475a-90f0-7c920b59c8cf/4-Install-packages.txt) it came from Fedora:
```
gvisor-tap-vsock-gvforwarder   x86_64  6:0.7.0-6.fc39                                                   updates-testing                                            1.8 M
```

Presumably because the [podman-next COPR](https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/build/6404120/)'s [most recent build](https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/build/6404120/)  is 6:0.7.0-1.20230914182756041449.main.39.g9ebd937 , i.e. the 1.2023.. is smaller than Fedora's -6 revision.

However, I don't know about these "6:" vs. "103:" prefixes. Are these epochs? But an epoch of "103:" sounds pretty absurd. Or are these `priority=` in the repo file? :thinking:
Our TF workaround hack landed, so feel free to retry all failed packit tests in recent PRs (I can't unfortunately)

obsoleted by https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20039 . f39 didn't have gvisor build earlier which is why the f39 test failed.

@martinpitt yes the 102/103 were set as absurdly high epochs for copr builds to override default packages. Is `priority` settable for copr repos when you enable them using `dnf copr enable` ? I can't seem to find any such option.
